intro: The clearest split between Amazon SES and Postmark is how they charge: Amazon SES uses a free tier plus paid plans starting around $50 per month, while Postmark uses usage-based pricing at $15/mo (10K emails). Amazon SES is AWS scalable transactional and bulk email sending service with SMTP and API access; it fits best for Transactional emails (receipts, alerts). Postmark is Fast transactional email delivery API focused on inbox placement with detailed bounce analytics; it fits best for Transactional emails (receipts, alerts). Pick Amazon SES for Transactional emails (receipts, alerts); pick Postmark for Transactional emails (receipts, alerts). Amazon SES is not for internal projects. Postmark is not for internal projects. Honest trade-offs: Amazon SES is weak for internal team communication, and Postmark is weak for internal team communication — match to your real scenario. Both sit under the Messaging API (Email / SMS / Voice) umbrella, so your final call depends on pricing transparency, integration effort, and which listed not-for situations you can actually tolerate.
quick_take: Amazon SES is for transactional emails (receipts, alerts); Postmark is for transactional emails (receipts, alerts); decide based on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Amazon SES if you want a free tier plus paid plans starting around $50 per month and your workload matches transactional emails (receipts, alerts).
verdict_b: Choose Postmark if you want usage-based pricing at $15/mo (10K emails) and your workload matches transactional emails (receipts, alerts).

faq.q: Is Amazon SES cheaper than Postmark?
faq.a: A direct comparison depends on your usage profile and which pricing model fits better. Amazon SES has a free tier with paid plans starting at $50 per month. Postmark uses usage-based pricing at $15/mo (10K emails).

faq.q: Can I migrate from Amazon SES to Postmark?
faq.a: Migration may be non-trivial: Amazon SES is rated high lock-in (SMTP-compatible, portable to other email providers). Plan for data export, re-integration of SDKs, and re-testing flows before cutover. Verify exportable formats and feature parity against your live use cases before committing.

Postmark scores higher on developer experience (5/5 vs 4/5 for Amazon SES). Evaluate against your own stack: read their docs, run a quick SDK spike, and check error handling. Self-assessing with a small prototype beats trusting any rating at face value.

faq.q: Is Postmark a good alternative to Amazon SES?
faq.a: Postmark sits in the same category as Amazon SES, so it can be a reasonable alternative for overlapping use cases. Postmark is best fit for transactional emails (receipts, alerts). Note: Postmark is not for internal projects.
